channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: In West Texas, an unlikely alliance stands against extending the border wall
item.description: An unusual coalition of people across the political spectrum have banded together to rally against a border wall in the Big Bend.
item.content:encoded:
An unusual coalition of people across the political spectrum have banded together to rally against a border wall in the Big Bend.

(Image credit: Carlos Morales)
item.dc:creator: Carlos Morales

item.title: Pope Leo warns that AI is becoming a new test of human dignity, work and power
item.description: In an encyclical, Pope Leo says AI poses a threat to human dignity.
item.content:encoded: In an encyclical, Pope Leo says AI poses a threat to human dignity.
item.dc:creator: Megan Williams

item.title: Noah Kahan on complications that come with fame, and being open about mental health
item.description: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Singer-songwriter Noah Kahan talks about what he calls the superpowers that come with depression and anxiety.
item.content:encoded: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Singer-songwriter Noah Kahan talks about what he calls the superpowers that come with depression and anxiety.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Martin

item.title: The Space Nerd Club discusses new findings from the James Webb telescope
item.description: New findings from the James Webb Space Telescope include cloudy weather on an exoplanet, and clues to the origins of one of Neptune's moons.
item.content:encoded: New findings from the James Webb Space Telescope include cloudy weather on an exoplanet, and clues to the origins of one of Neptune's moons.
item.dc:creator: Scott Detrow

item.title: How Uyghurs from China helped Syrian rebels overthrow Bashar al-Assad
item.description: Motivated to avenge their families in China, Uyghur mercenaries became valued fighting partners for Syria's rebels. Their participation helped Syria's new president ultimately win Syria's civil war.
item.content:encoded: Motivated to avenge their families in China, Uyghur mercenaries became valued fighting partners for Syria's rebels. Their participation helped Syria's new president ultimately win Syria's civil war.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: 'Cemetery citizens' are restoring marginalized burial grounds in Connecticut
item.description: There's a volunteer movement to rehab overgrown cemeteries. We visit one in Connecticut, where "cemetery citizens" provide TLC to neglected plots.
item.content:encoded: There's a volunteer movement to rehab overgrown cemeteries. We visit one in Connecticut, where "cemetery citizens" provide TLC to neglected plots.
item.dc:creator: Meg Dalton

item.title: Why Israel is unhappy with the latest U.S. negotiations with Iran
item.description: Israeli officials have called the emerging U.S. deal with Iran a "bad" deal, over concerns that it does not force Iran to give up its nuclear program at the start.
item.content:encoded: Israeli officials have called the emerging U.S. deal with Iran a "bad" deal, over concerns that it does not force Iran to give up its nuclear program at the start.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: The removal of a whale mural in Dallas ahead of World Cup has caused a stir
item.description: A mural of whales promoting conservation was covered up in Dallas to make way for a new mural promoting the FIFA World Cup, which kicks off next month. But the project has stirred up controversy.
item.content:encoded: A mural of whales promoting conservation was covered up in Dallas to make way for a new mural promoting the FIFA World Cup, which kicks off next month. But the project has stirred up controversy.
item.dc:creator: Marcheta Fornoff

item.title: Brennan Center CEO calls some of Trump's moves 'corrupt.' What can be done about it?
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks to Michael Waldman, president of the Brennan Center for Justice, about recent moves from the Trump administration and why he thinks they amount to "epic corruption in plain sight."
item.content:encoded: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks to Michael Waldman, president of the Brennan Center for Justice, about recent moves from the Trump administration and why he thinks they amount to "epic corruption in plain sight."
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly

item.title: An older kid stepped up to save him from bullies — and changed his life
item.description: As a sixth-grader, Darrell Barber was the new kid at school and a target for bullies. An eighth-grader named Marcus became his protector.
item.content:encoded: As a sixth-grader, Darrell Barber was the new kid at school and a target for bullies. An eighth-grader named Marcus became his protector.
item.dc:creator: Mallory Yu
